title: requests — queued jobs
description: Three queued jobs. All models re-hydrated from DB on handle (no stale-payload risk). ShouldBeUnique on jobs whose duplicate execution would over-send.

jobs.description: Dispatched from ShimApprovalRequestedListener after AccessRequestSubmitted fires. Iterates the resolved approvers set for the request's requirements + dispatches AccessRequestSubmittedNotification to each. Idempotent via (instance_id, approver_id) unique key — duplicate runs no-op. Fires AccessRequestApproverNotified per successful delivery for the analytics pipeline.

Dispatched when a terminal event fires (AccessRequestApproved / Rejected / Expired). Delivers the matching notification (AccessRequestApprovedNotification / RejectedNotification / ExpiredNotification) to the requester. Unique key per (instance_id, decision_kind) prevents duplicate notifications on transient failures.

Daily sweeper. Scans approval_instances where `action_key='access.grants.create'` AND status='pending' AND (`subject_id` no longer resolves to a live row OR the resource_type is no longer in the registry). Auto-rejects with `reason='resource_no_longer_exists'` via workflow/approvals::withdraw with a system actor. Batched at 500 per run to avoid blocking the queue. Idempotent by construction — rejected instances are skipped on the next sweep.

notifications.description: Notification-only work. Isolated from default queue so a burst of decisions (bulk approve on enterprise) does not stall the CriticalPath default queue.

retention.description: Batched cleanup jobs. Runs off-hours per schedule.json. Shared with other modules' retention work.

notes: There is INTENTIONALLY no `MaterializeGrantJob` — grant materialisation runs INLINE in AccessRequestGrantMaterializer on the default queue synchronous with the ApprovalExecuted event. This preserves the invariant 'if AccessRequestApproved fired, the grant WILL exist within seconds' — users retry the resource fetch expecting it to work.

If materialisation ever needs to become truly asynchronous (e.g. because the grant creation involves a slow external call), the correct move is to fire an `AccessRequestGrantPending` event first and let the materialiser be a proper job — don't repurpose Cleanup or NotifyApprovers.
